title: Structure of relatively bi-exact group von Neumann algebras
overall_summary: The paper develops a von Neumann algebraic bidual method for upgrading proper proximality relative to a boundary piece into a direct-sum decomposition whose summands are either fully properly proximal or amenable relative to the subalgebras generating the boundary piece. The core technical input is a computation inside the normal bidual of B(L^2 M), together with a construction of u.c.p. maps from basic constructions ⟨M,e_{M_i}⟩ into the relative small-at-infinity bidual when the inclusions M_i ⊂ M are mixing and admit bounded Pimsner-Popa bases. Its main upgrading theorem, Theorem 1.1, is directly relevant to any proof strategy that first establishes proper proximality relative to link/star/parabolic subalgebras and then uses irreducibility or malnormality-type properties to rule out relative amenability to those subalgebras. The paper also proves that relatively bi-exact groups with almost malnormal peripheral subgroups have von Neumann algebras whose subalgebras satisfy such a decomposition, and applies the mechanism to free products.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Definition 2.1 and group example: bounded Pimsner-Popa bases
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let N ⊂ M be an inclusion of separable finite von Neumann algebras. A Pimsner-Popa basis for M over N is a family {m_j}_{j∈J} ⊂ M such that E_N(m_j^* m_k)=δ_{j,k} p_j for projections p_j∈N, and L^2(M)=⊕_{j∈J} m_j L^2(N), equivalently every x∈M has a unique expansion x=Σ_j m_j E_N(m_j^* x). The inclusion N ⊂ M is said to admit a bounded Pimsner-Popa basis if such a basis can be chosen with sup_j ||m_j||<∞. In the group case N=LΛ ⊂ LΓ=M for a subgroup Λ<Γ, choosing left coset representatives Γ=⊔_j t_jΛ gives a bounded basis m_j=λ_{t_j}, consisting of unitaries.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The definition is recorded for later use. In the group case, ℓ^2(Γ) decomposes as the orthogonal direct sum of the subspaces λ_{t_j}ℓ^2(Λ). The conditional expectation E_{LΛ} kills group elements outside Λ, so E_{LΛ}(λ_{t_j}^*λ_{t_k})=0 for j≠k and equals 1 for j=k. Thus the coset representatives give the required orthogonal right LΛ-module decomposition and the basis is uniformly bounded because each element is unitary.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Theorem 2.2: Popa intertwining-by-bimodules criterion
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let (M,τ) be a tracial von Neumann algebra, let P ⊂ pMp and Q ⊂ M be von Neumann subalgebras, with p a projection in M. The following are equivalent. First, there exist projections p_0∈P and q_0∈Q, a ∗-homomorphism θ:p_0Pp_0→q_0Qq_0, and a nonzero partial isometry v∈q_0Mp_0 such that θ(x)v=vx for every x∈p_0Pp_0. Second, there is no sequence of unitaries u_n∈U(P) such that ||E_Q(x^* u_n y)||_2→0 for all x,y∈pM. When these equivalent conditions hold, the notation P≺_M Q is used, meaning that a corner of P embeds into Q inside M.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The paper cites this standard theorem of Popa and does not reprove it. Its operational use is the second criterion: to prove P does not intertwine into Q, one constructs unitaries in P whose Q-expectations vanish after multiplication on the left and right by arbitrary elements of M. Conversely, absence of such a vanishing sequence produces a finite P-Q subbimodule and hence the partial-isometry implementation in the first condition.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Section 2.3 / [OP10, Theorem 2.1]: state criterion for relative amenability
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let P,Q ⊂ M be von Neumann subalgebras of a finite von Neumann algebra (M,τ), and let ⟨M,e_Q⟩ be the basic construction generated by M and the Jones projection e_Q:L^2(M)→L^2(Q). The paper recalls that P is amenable relative to Q inside M iff there exists a sequence ξ_n∈L^2(⟨M,e_Q⟩) such that ⟨xξ_n,ξ_n⟩→τ(x) for every x∈M and ||yξ_n−ξ_n y||_2→0 for every y∈P. Equivalently, P is amenable relative to Q inside M iff there exists a P-central state Ω on ⟨M,e_Q⟩ whose restriction to M is normal and which is faithful on Z(P′∩M).
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: This criterion is cited from Ozawa-Popa and is not proved in the paper. The paper repeatedly uses the state formulation in the following way: once a P-central state on a basic construction is constructed, its support on Z(P′∩M) gives a central summand of P that is amenable relative to Q. In Theorem 1.1, the constructed state need not be faithful on all of Z(P′∩M), so the support projection of its restriction to the relative commutant center is taken to isolate the relative amenability summand.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Remark 2.3: compactness consequence of mixing inclusions
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a diffuse finite von Neumann algebra and N ⊂ M a diffuse von Neumann subalgebra. The inclusion N ⊂ M is mixing if L^2(M⊖N) is mixing as an N-N bimodule, equivalently if for any sequence of unitaries u_n∈U(N) converging weakly to 0 one has ||E_N(x u_n y)||_2→0 for all x,y∈M⊖N. When M and N are diffuse, the paper notes that one may replace such sequences of unitaries by arbitrary weakly-null sequences in N. If N ⊂ M is mixing, then for x,y∈M with at least one of x,y lying in M⊖N, the operator e_N x J y J e_N is compact as a map from the normed space M to L^2(M). Equivalently, for arbitrary x,y∈M, the difference e_N x J y J e_N − e_N E_N(x) J E_N(y) J e_N is compact in this sense. The paper records examples: M_1 and M_2 are mixing subalgebras of the tracial free product M_1∗M_2 when the factors are diffuse, and LΛ ⊂ LΓ is mixing when Λ<Γ is almost malnormal.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The compactness assertion follows from the mixing condition: a bounded sequence in N that is weakly null is sent by the coefficient map a↦E_N(xay) to a norm-null sequence whenever x or y has zero N-expectation. This is the usual compactness criterion for such bimodule coefficient maps in the diffuse setting. The examples are cited from Jolissaint for free products and Boutonnet-Carderi for almost malnormal subgroup inclusions.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.2: boundary piece generated by a join of commuting Jones projections
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a finite von Neumann algebra and let M_i ⊂ M, i=1,...,n, be von Neumann subalgebras such that the Jones projections e_{M_i}:L^2(M)→L^2(M_i) pairwise commute. Let e_0=∨_{i=1}^n e_{M_i}. Let X be the hereditary C∗-subalgebra of B(L^2 M) generated by operators x J y J e_0 with x,y∈M. Let Y be the hereditary C∗-subalgebra of B(L^2 M) generated by all x J y J e_{M_i}, with x,y∈M and i=1,...,n. Then X=Y.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Since 0≤e_{M_i}≤e_0, each e_{M_i} lies in X, hence Y⊂X. Conversely, because the projections e_{M_i} commute, finite joins can be written using sums and meets: e_i∨e_j=e_i+e_j−e_i∧e_j, and e_i∧e_j≤e_i. Since hereditary C∗-subalgebras contain positive suboperators dominated by their positive elements, this shows e_0=∨_i e_{M_i} lies in Y. Because M and JMJ lie in the multiplier algebra of Y, xJyJ e_0 also lies in the closed left ideal generated by Y for all x,y∈M, giving X⊂Y.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.3: approximate unit for a subalgebra-generated boundary piece
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: In the setting of Lemma 3.2, let e_0=∨_{i=1}^n e_{M_i} and let X be the M-boundary piece generated by x J y J e_0, x,y∈M. Let F range over finite subsets of U(M), ordered by inclusion, and define e_F=∨_{u,v∈F} u J v J e_0 J v^* J u^*. Then {e_F}_F is an approximate unit for X.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Each e_F is in X because it is dominated by the finite sum of the conjugates uJvJ e_0 Jv^*Ju^*. If 1∈F then e_0 e_F=e_0, and if u_0,v_0∈F then e_F(u_0Jv_0J e_0)=u_0Jv_0J e_0; similarly right multiplication by e_F fixes the corresponding generators. Since every element of M is a linear combination of unitaries, this verifies that the net acts as a two-sided approximate unit on the generators of the hereditary C∗-algebra and hence on all of X.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Remark 3.4: induced boundary pieces and permanence observations
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M and Q be finite von Neumann algebras, let X be an M-boundary piece, and let N ⊂ pMp be a von Neumann subalgebra for a nonzero projection p∈M. Define E_N=Ad(e_N)∘Ad(pJpJ):B(L^2 M)→B(L^2 N). The paper recalls that E_N(K_X(M))⊂B(L^2 N) forms an N-boundary piece, called the induced N-boundary piece and denoted X_N. It also records: if N is properly proximal relative to X inside M, then zN is properly proximal relative to X inside M for every nonzero central projection z of N; if N is properly proximal relative to X inside M, then N has no amenable direct summand; and proper proximality is stable under direct sums, so every finite von Neumann algebra Q has a maximal properly proximal direct summand Qf for some central projection f∈Z(Q).
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The induced boundary-piece assertion is cited from [DKEP22, Remark 6.3]. For central summands, a forbidden central state for zN can be composed with compression by z to produce one for N. For the no-amenable-direct-summand assertion, if qN were amenable for a nonzero central q, one could take a qN-central state on B(L^2(qN)) and compose with Ad(q)∘Ad(e_N) to obtain an N-central state on the relevant small-at-infinity operator system, normal on M, contradicting relative proper proximality. Stability under direct sums gives the maximal properly proximal summand by taking the supremum of central projections supporting properly proximal direct summands.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.5: bidual formulation of relative proper proximality
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a separable tracial von Neumann algebra and X an M-boundary piece. For a C∗-subalgebra A⊂B(L^2 M) with M and JMJ in its multiplier algebra, let A_J^♯ be the subspace of A^∗ consisting of functionals whose left and right M-actions and JMJ-actions are separately normal; then (A_J^♯)^∗ is a von Neumann algebra. Define the bidual relative small-at-infinity operator system by ~S_X(M)={T∈(B(L^2M)_J^♯)^∗ : [T,a]∈(K_X(M)_J^♯)^∗ for every a∈JMJ}. Then M is properly proximal relative to X iff there is no M-central state φ on ~S_X(M) whose restriction to M is normal.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The result is cited from [DKEP22, Lemma 8.5]. The point is that the usual operator system S_X(M) can be replaced by a normal bidual model without changing the existence of central states normal on M. One direction restricts a central state on the bidual to the original operator system. The other direction passes from a central state on S_X(M) to a weak∗ cluster/extension in the normal bidual, using separability and the normal-part construction so that commutators with JMJ land in the bidual of K_X(M).

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.7: approximate units identify the identity in the normal bidual
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a finite von Neumann algebra and X an M-boundary piece. Let X_0⊂K_X(M) be a C∗-subalgebra with approximate unit {e_n}_{n∈I}. Assume X_0 is dense in K_X^{∞,1}(M) for the ||·||_{∞,1}-norm and that the canonical image ι(e_n)∈B(L^2M)^{∗∗} commutes with the normal support projection p_nor for every n. Then any weak∗ limit of ι_nor(e_n)=p_nor ι(e_n) p_nor is the identity of the von Neumann algebra (K_X(M)_J^♯)^∗.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The image ι_nor(K_X(M)) is weak∗ dense in (K_X(M)_J^♯)^∗, and functionals in K_X(M)_J^♯ are continuous for the ||·||_{∞,1}-topology; hence ι_nor(X_0) is weak∗ dense. If e is a weak∗ limit of ι_nor(e_n), then for T∈X_0 one has e ι_nor(T)=lim_n p_nor ι(e_n T)p_nor=ι_nor(T), and similarly ι_nor(T)e=ι_nor(T). By weak∗ density, e acts as the identity on all of (K_X(M)_J^♯)^∗.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.8: Jones projections commute with the normal support projection
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let N ⊂ M be an inclusion of finite von Neumann algebras, and let e_N∈B(L^2M) be the orthogonal projection onto L^2(N). Let p_nor∈B(L^2M)^{∗∗} be the central support projection defining the normal part for the M and JMJ actions. Then ι(e_N) commutes with p_nor.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Represent B(L^2M)^{∗∗} on a Hilbert space H. A vector ξ lies in the range of p_nor exactly when the vector states x↦⟨ι(x)ξ,ξ⟩ are normal on both M and JMJ. If ξ is in this range, then the vector state associated with ι(e_N)ξ is x↦⟨ι(E_N(x))ξ,ξ⟩ on M, hence normal; the same argument works for JMJ. Thus ι(e_N)p_nor has range in p_norH, giving ι(e_N)p_nor=p_norι(e_N)p_nor. Taking adjoints yields commutation.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.9: mixing and a bounded basis decompose q_K^⊥ q_X
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let N ⊂ M be a mixing inclusion of finite von Neumann algebras admitting a Pimsner-Popa basis {m_k}_{k≥0} with m_k∈M. Let X_N be the M-boundary piece associated with N, i.e. generated by xJyJ e_N for x,y∈M. Let q_K be the identity of (K(L^2M)_J^♯)^∗ and q_{X_N} the identity of (K_{X_N}(M)_J^♯)^∗. With the notation of the paper, define p_{k,l}=q_K^⊥ ι_nor(m_k Jm_l^*J e_N Jm_lJ m_k^*) in the normal bidual. Then the p_{k,l} are pairwise orthogonal projections and their orthogonal sum satisfies Σ_{k,l} p_{k,l}=q_K^⊥ q_{X_N}.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The proof first uses mixing to obtain the compactness relation e_N xJyJ e_N − e_N E_N(x)J E_N(y)J e_N ∈ K(M) in the sense of compact maps from M to L^2(M). After multiplying by q_K^⊥, compact terms vanish. Combining this with the Pimsner-Popa orthogonality E_N(m_k^*m_{k′})=δ_{k,k′}q_k shows that the p_{k,l} multiply as p_{k,l}p_{k′,l′}=0 unless k=k′ and l=l′, and are projections. Next, let A be the C∗-algebra generated by {m_k a : a∈N,k≥0}, and let X_0 be the hereditary C∗-subalgebra generated by xJyJ e_N with x,y∈A. The basis expansion makes A weakly dense in M, and the paper checks that X_0 is ||·||_{∞,1}-dense in K_{X_N}^{∞,1}(M), hence ι_nor(X_0) is weak∗ dense in the bidual. Finally, on dense generators such as m_k Jm_lJ aJbJ e_N and their adjoints, the sum Σ_{k,l}p_{k,l} acts as the identity after q_K^⊥; therefore it equals q_K^⊥ q_{X_N}.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.10: bidual identity for a boundary piece generated by finitely many commuting subalgebras
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a finite von Neumann algebra and M_i ⊂ M, i=1,...,n, von Neumann subalgebras with pairwise commuting Jones projections e_{M_i}. Let X be the M-boundary piece associated with the family {M_i}_{i=1}^n, and let X_i be the boundary piece associated with M_i alone. Let q_X be the identity of (K_X(M)_J^♯)^∗ and q_i the identity of (K_{X_i}(M)_J^♯)^∗. Then q_X=∨_{i=1}^n q_i.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The inequality q_X≥q_i is immediate because each X_i is contained in X. For the converse, choose an increasing sequence of finite sets F_n⊂U(M) whose union generates M as a von Neumann algebra, and put e_n=∨_{u,v∈F_n}uJvJ(∨_i e_{M_i})Jv^*Ju^*. By Lemma 3.3 these form an approximate unit for X, and by Lemma 3.7 their images ι_nor(e_n) converge weak∗ to q_X. But for each n, ι_nor(e_n)≤∨_i q_i because e_n is the join of finite conjugates lying in the individual boundary pieces. Hence q_X≤∨_i q_i.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Lemma 3.11: compression to a subalgebra on the bidual small-at-infinity systems
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a finite von Neumann algebra, let N ⊂ pMp be a von Neumann subalgebra for a nonzero projection p∈M, and define E=Ad(e_N)∘Ad(pJpJ):B(L^2M)→B(L^2N). The paper proves that E sends the absolute small-at-infinity operator system S(M) into S(N). Moreover, there is a u.c.p. map ~E:~S(M)→~S(N) between the corresponding bidual small-at-infinity systems such that ~E restricted to M agrees with the conditional expectation from M onto N.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: For a∈N, the relation pJpJ e_N J_NaJ_N=JaJ pJpJ e_N gives [E(T),J_NaJ_N]=E([T,JaJ]). If T∈S(M), then [T,JaJ] lies in K^{∞,1}(M), and E is ||·||_{∞,1}-continuous, so E([T,JaJ]) lies in K^{∞,1}(N); hence E(T)∈S(N). On preduals, E^∗ sends functionals separately normal for N and JNJ to functionals separately normal for M and JMJ, and similarly for the compact ideals. Taking adjoints gives the u.c.p. map on the bidual systems. On M, the formula is exactly compression by e_N, i.e. the conditional expectation.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Theorem 1.1: upgrading relative proper proximality to proper proximality plus relative amenability summands
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M be a diffuse finite von Neumann algebra. Let M_i ⊂ M, i=1,...,n, be diffuse von Neumann subalgebras such that the Jones projections e_{M_i} pairwise commute; assume each inclusion M_i ⊂ M admits a bounded Pimsner-Popa basis and is mixing. Let p∈M be a projection and let A ⊂ pMp be a von Neumann subalgebra. Let X be the M-boundary piece associated with the family {M_i}_{i=1}^n. If A is properly proximal relative to X inside M, then there exist projections f_0∈Z(A) and f_i∈Z(A′∩pMp), i=1,...,n, such that Af_0 is properly proximal, Af_i is amenable relative to M_i inside M for every i=1,...,n, and Σ_{i=0}^n f_i=p.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Let f∈Z(A) be chosen so that Af^⊥ is the maximal properly proximal direct summand of A. If f=0, take f_0=p and there is nothing to prove. Otherwise Af is still properly proximal relative to X, has no amenable direct summand, and has no properly proximal direct summand. By the bidual characterization, there is an Af-central state μ on ~S(Af) normal on Af; by a maximality argument μ may be chosen faithful on Z(Af). Use Lemma 3.11 to get ~E:~S(M)→~S(Af), and set φ=μ∘~E. Then φ is Af-central and φ|_{fMf} is faithful normal. Let q_K be the identity of the compact bidual and q_X the identity of the X-boundary bidual. If φ(q_K)>0, restricting to the compact bidual gives an Af-central state on B(L^2M), hence an amenable direct summand of Af, contradiction. If φ(q_X^⊥)>0, normalizing φ∘Ad(q_X^⊥) gives an Af-central state on ~S_X(M) normal on fMf, contradicting relative proper proximality. Thus φ(q_X q_K^⊥)=1. By Lemma 3.10, q_X=∨_i q_i, where q_i is the identity for the boundary piece generated by M_i; hence some q_i has positive φ-mass after q_K^⊥. For such an i, the key claim constructs a u.c.p. map Φ_i:⟨M,e_{M_i}⟩→q_K^⊥ q_i ~S(M) q_i satisfying Φ_i(x)=q_K^⊥q_i x for all x∈M. To prove the claim, take a bounded Pimsner-Popa basis {m_k} for M over M_i. Define finite-rank cutdowns ψ_n on ⟨M,e_{M_i}⟩ by ψ_n(T)=(Σ_{k≤n}m_k e_{M_i}m_k^*)T(Σ_{l≤n}m_l e_{M_i}m_l^*). On the algebra A_0=span{m_k a e_{M_i}m_l^*:a∈M_i}, define a map φ_0 using the bidual projection e_i^b=Σ_k ι_nor(Jm_kJ e_{M_i} Jm_k^*J) by φ_0(m_r a e_{M_i}m_l^*)=q_K^⊥ι_nor(m_r a)e_i^b ι_nor(m_l^*). Mixing gives the crucial identity q_K^⊥ e_i^b ι_nor(x)e_i^b=q_K^⊥ι_nor(E_{M_i}(x))e_i^b, because all off-expectation terms are compact and disappear after q_K^⊥; this makes φ_0 a ∗-homomorphism. Boundedness of the basis is used in the estimate |⟨φ_0(Σ_r m_{k_r}a_r e_{M_i}m_{l_r}^*)ξ,η⟩|≤(sup_k||m_k||^2)||Σ_r m_{k_r}a_r e_{M_i}m_{l_r}^*||, obtained using pairwise orthogonal projections coming from mixing, so φ_0 extends by norm continuity. A further computation with unitaries in M_i shows φ_0(A_0) commutes with JMJ modulo the compact bidual, hence lies in q_K^⊥~S(M). Taking weak∗ cluster points of φ_0∘ψ_n gives Φ_i, and Lemma 3.9 gives Φ_i(x)=q_K^⊥q_i x on M. Now ν_i=φ∘Φ_i is an Af-central state on ⟨M,e_{M_i}⟩, normal on fMf after normalization when φ(q_iq_K^⊥)>0. Let f_i be the support projection of ν_i restricted to Z((Af)′∩fMf); by the relative amenability criterion, Af_i is amenable relative to M_i. Repeating for all i with positive mass gives projections f_i. Finally, φ(q_i f_i^⊥)=φ(Φ_i(f_i^⊥))=ν_i(f_i^⊥)=0, so φ(∨_i f_i)≥φ(∨_i q_i f_i)=φ(∨_i q_i)=1. Since φ|_{fMf} is faithful, ∨_i f_i=f. Orthogonalizing these central relative-commutant projections and setting f_0=f^⊥ gives the desired sum decomposition.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Theorem 3.12: relatively bi-exact groups give relative proper proximality for subalgebras without amenable summands
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let Γ be a nonamenable countable group that is bi-exact relative to a finite family of subgroups {Λ_i}_{i∈I}. Let M=LΓ, and let X be the M-boundary piece associated with the family {LΛ_i}_{i∈I}. If A ⊂ pMp is a von Neumann subalgebra, for a nonzero projection p∈M, and A has no amenable direct summand, then A is properly proximal relative to the induced A-boundary piece X_A obtained from X by compression to A.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Embed ℓ^∞(Γ) diagonally into B(ℓ^2Γ). Under this embedding, the relative ideal c_0(Γ,{Λ_i}) is sent into the boundary piece X, and the group-level relative small-at-infinity algebra S_X(Γ) embeds Γ-equivariantly into S_X(M). Therefore there is a ∗-homomorphism S_X(Γ)⋊_rΓ→B(ℓ^2Γ) whose image lies in S_X(M). If A were not properly proximal relative to X_A, the paper uses the boundary-piece formalism to obtain a nonzero central projection p_0∈Z(A) and an Ap_0-bimodular u.c.p. map from S_{X_A}(A) to Ap_0. Composing the crossed-product map with compression to A and then with this u.c.p. map gives a u.c.p. map S_X(Γ)⋊_rΓ→Ap_0, and hence a state from the vector state of p_0. In the GNS representation of this state, a Boutonnet-Carderi argument produces a projection q of full state mass and a normal unital ∗-homomorphism LΓ→qπ(S_X(Γ)⋊_rΓ)″q extending the regular representation. Relative bi-exactness means Γ acts amenably on the relative compactification, so S_X(Γ)⋊_rΓ is nuclear. Nuclearity extends the above representation to a u.c.p. map from B(ℓ^2Γ). Composing with the GNS state yields an Ap_0-central state on B(ℓ^2Γ), showing Ap_0 is amenable, contradicting the assumption that A has no amenable direct summand.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Theorem 1.2: subalgebra decomposition for relatively bi-exact groups with almost malnormal subgroups
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let Γ be a countable group with a finite family of almost malnormal subgroups {Λ_i}_{i=1}^n, meaning each Λ_i satisfies gΛ_i g^{-1}∩Λ_i finite for all g∈Γ\Λ_i. Suppose Γ is bi-exact relative to {Λ_i}_{i=1}^n. Then for every von Neumann subalgebra A⊂LΓ there exist a projection p∈Z(A) and projections p_i∈Z((Ap)′∩pLΓp), i=1,...,n, such that ∨_{i=1}^n p_i=p, each Ap_i is amenable relative to LΛ_i inside LΓ, and Ap^⊥ is properly proximal.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: For subgroup inclusions LΛ_i⊂LΓ, bounded Pimsner-Popa bases are given by unitary coset representatives. The Jones projections e_{LΛ_i} commute on ℓ^2Γ. Almost malnormality of Λ_i implies the inclusion LΛ_i⊂LΓ is mixing. Theorem 3.12 supplies relative proper proximality, with respect to the boundary piece generated by the LΛ_i, on the nonamenable/nontrivial part of any subalgebra. Applying Theorem 1.1 to that family of mixing subalgebras yields a decomposition into a properly proximal summand and summands amenable relative to the LΛ_i, which is exactly the stated conclusion.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Proposition 3.13: free product deformation gives relative proper proximality with respect to word length
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M_1 and M_2 be finite von Neumann algebras and let M=M_1∗M_2 be their tracial free product. Consider the free product malleable deformation on ~M=M∗LF_2; its associated maps satisfy E_M∘α_t=P_0+Σ_{n≥1}(sin(πt)/(πt))^{2n}P_n, where P_n is the orthogonal projection onto the subspace of L^2(M) spanned by reduced words of length n. Let X_F be the hereditary C∗-algebra generated by the projections {P_n}_{n≥0}. If A⊂M is a nonamenable von Neumann subalgebra, then there exists a central projection p∈Z(A) such that Ap is amenable and Ap^⊥ is properly proximal relative to X_F.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The paper invokes [DKEP22, Proposition 9.1], which gives an M-bimodular u.c.p. map from (M^{op})′∩B(L^2~M⊖L^2M) into ~S_{X_F}(M). Since L^2~M⊖L^2M is isomorphic to L^2M⊗K as an M-M bimodule for a right M-module K, this map may be restricted to B(L^2M)⊗id_K. Let p∈Z(A) be maximal with Ap amenable. If Ap^⊥ were not properly proximal relative to X_F, an Ap^⊥-central state on ~S_{X_F}(M), normal on Ap^⊥, could be composed with the above u.c.p. map to produce an A-central state on B(L^2M) supported on a nonzero central projection q≤p^⊥. That would make Aq amenable, contradicting maximality of p.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Theorem 5.1: structural decomposition for free products of exact group von Neumann algebras
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M_i=LΓ_i for countable exact groups Γ_i, i=1,2, and let M=M_1∗M_2 be the tracial free product. For every von Neumann subalgebra A⊂M there exist projections p_1,p_2,p_3∈Z(A′∩M) such that Ap_i≺_M M_i for i=1,2, Ap_3 is amenable, and A(∨_{i=1}^3 p_i)^⊥ is properly proximal.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: The free product group Γ_1∗Γ_2 is bi-exact relative to {Γ_1,Γ_2}, and the Jones projections e_{M_1},e_{M_2} commute. Thus Theorem 3.12 and Theorem 1.1 give central relative-commutant projections f_1,f_2 such that Af_i is amenable relative to M_i, plus a properly proximal remainder. For each i, take p_i to be the nonamenable part of Af_i after removing its maximal amenable central summand p_i′. Ioana's lemma for free products says that if a subalgebra is amenable relative to M_i, then either it embeds into M_i inside M or it is amenable. Hence the nonamenable parts satisfy Ap_i≺_M M_i, while the removed parts combine into the amenable projection p_3=p_1′+p_2′.

key_theorems_lemmas.label: Corollary 1.3: proper-proximal absorption in free products
key_theorems_lemmas.statement: Let M_i=LΓ_i where Γ_i are countable exact groups, and let M=M_1∗M_2 be the tracial free product. Let A⊂M be a von Neumann subalgebra such that A∩M_1 is diffuse. If A has no properly proximal direct summand, then A⊂M_1.
key_theorems_lemmas.proof_sketch: Since A has no properly proximal direct summand, Theorem 3.12 and Theorem 1.1 provide projections f_1,f_2∈Z(A′∩M) with f_1+f_2=1 such that Af_i is amenable relative to M_i. If Af_2 were nonamenable, Ioana's lemma would give Af_2≺_M M_2. But because A∩M_1 is diffuse, choose trace-zero unitaries u_n∈A∩M_1 converging weakly to 0. A free product normal-form computation gives ||E_{M_2}(x u_n f_2 y)||_2→0 for all x,y∈M, contradicting Popa's intertwining criterion for Af_2≺_M M_2. Thus A is amenable relative to M_1. The Boutonnet-Houdayer absorption theorem then applies: if A∩M_1 is diffuse and A is amenable relative to M_1 inside M_1∗M_2, then A⊂M_1.

proof_techniques.name: Boundary pieces generated by finitely many subalgebras
proof_techniques.description: The paper packages a finite family of subalgebras M_i⊂M into a single M-boundary piece X, the hereditary C∗-subalgebra generated by xJyJ e_{M_i}. When the Jones projections e_{M_i} commute, this boundary piece is equivalently generated by the single projection ∨_i e_{M_i}; it has explicit approximate units built from finite unitary conjugates uJvJ(∨_i e_{M_i})Jv^*Ju^*. In the normal bidual, the identity q_X of the boundary ideal decomposes as the join q_X=∨_i q_i of the identities for the individual boundary pieces.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: For graph products, natural candidates for M_i are parabolic, link, or star subalgebras. If their conditional expectations/Jones projections commute, Lemmas 3.2, 3.3, and 3.10 provide a way to combine several relative boundary pieces and later force any obstructing central state to concentrate on one of them.

proof_techniques.name: Bidual localization of a non-proper-proximal central state
proof_techniques.description: The upgrade proof works in ~S(M), the normal bidual small-at-infinity system. Given a central state φ witnessing failure of full proper proximality on the non-properly-proximal summand, the proof analyzes its mass on two bidual projections: q_K, the identity of the compact ideal, and q_X, the identity of the relative boundary ideal. Mass on q_K would produce an amenable direct summand; mass on q_X^⊥ would contradict relative proper proximality. Hence φ is supported on q_X q_K^⊥, and q_X=∨q_i forces positive mass on at least one individual boundary component q_i.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: If one proves that a graph product von Neumann algebra is properly proximal relative to a family of parabolic/link subalgebras, this localization converts any failure of full proper proximality into a state supported on one parabolic component. The remaining task is then to turn that state into relative amenability and rule it out using irreducibility or normal-form arguments.

proof_techniques.name: Constructing basic-construction maps from mixing plus a bounded Pimsner-Popa basis
proof_techniques.description: The central technical claim in Theorem 1.1 constructs, for a mixing inclusion N⊂M with bounded Pimsner-Popa basis {m_k}, a u.c.p. map Φ:⟨M,e_N⟩→q_K^⊥ q_N ~S(M) q_N satisfying Φ(x)=q_K^⊥q_N x for all x∈M. The construction first defines a ∗-homomorphism on the algebraic span of m_k a e_N m_l^* using the bidual projection Σ_k ι_nor(Jm_kJ e_N Jm_k^*J). Mixing kills off-expectation terms modulo q_K, and boundedness of the basis gives the norm estimate needed to extend the map. Weak∗ limits of finite basis cutdowns then yield the desired u.c.p. map on the full basic construction.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: This is the key mechanism for upgrading relative proper proximality. To use it for graph products, one should identify subalgebras N, likely parabolic/link/star subalgebras, for which N⊂M is mixing or has an analogous compactness property, and construct bounded Pimsner-Popa bases from reduced graph-product words.

proof_techniques.name: Central-support decomposition via relative amenability states
proof_techniques.description: After a central state on ~S(M) is pushed through the u.c.p. map from a basic construction, the result is an A-central state on ⟨M,e_{M_i}⟩. Its restriction to Z(A′∩M) may not be faithful, so the paper takes its support projection f_i in the center of the relative commutant. The Ozawa-Popa criterion then gives that Af_i is amenable relative to M_i. Faithfulness of the original state on the center of the non-properly-proximal summand is used to prove that the union of these support projections covers that whole summand.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: For proving the whole graph product algebra M is properly proximal, apply the decomposition to A=M. If all possible relative amenability summands M f_i relative to the chosen proper parabolic subalgebras can be excluded, the decomposition leaves only the properly proximal summand.

proof_techniques.name: Relative bi-exactness implies relative proper proximality through nuclear crossed products
proof_techniques.description: Theorem 3.12 adapts the group-level relative bi-exact boundary to the von Neumann algebra boundary piece generated by subgroup von Neumann algebras. The diagonal embedding of ℓ^∞Γ into B(ℓ^2Γ) sends the group relative ideal into the von Neumann boundary piece, yielding a map from the reduced crossed product S_X(Γ)⋊_rΓ into S_X(LΓ). If a subalgebra without amenable summands were not relatively properly proximal, an equivariant u.c.p. map and a Boutonnet-Carderi GNS argument would produce a representation of LΓ inside a corner of the crossed product bidual. Nuclearity of the crossed product, coming from relative bi-exactness, extends this to B(ℓ^2Γ), yielding an invariant state and hence amenability, contradiction.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: For graph products of groups, or for graph-product von Neumann algebras with an available analogue of relative bi-exactness, this gives a template for establishing the initial relative proper proximality before invoking the upgrade theorem.

proof_techniques.name: Free-product deformation as a source of relative boundary pieces
proof_techniques.description: For M=M_1∗M_2, the paper recalls the free-product malleable deformation whose compression to M is diagonal on reduced word length subspaces, with coefficients (sin(πt)/(πt))^{2n}. The hereditary C∗-algebra generated by the word-length projections P_n is used as a boundary piece X_F. An M-bimodular u.c.p. map from the complement bimodule of the deformation into ~S_{X_F}(M) then implies that every nonamenable subalgebra has a direct summand properly proximal relative to X_F, up to an amenable summand.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: Graph products have reduced-word length structures and can often be decomposed using free-product-like normal forms. A comparable word-length boundary or deformation could provide the initial relative proper proximality needed before the bidual upgrade.

proof_techniques.name: Using weakly-null unitaries to rule out embedding into the wrong free-product factor
proof_techniques.description: In Corollary 1.3, once a summand is amenable relative to M_2, Ioana's dichotomy would force it to intertwine into M_2 unless it is amenable. The paper rules out this intertwining when A∩M_1 is diffuse by choosing weakly-null trace-zero unitaries u_n∈A∩M_1 and using free-product normal forms to show E_{M_2}(x u_n y)→0 for all x,y∈M, contradicting Popa's intertwining criterion.
proof_techniques.applicability_to_problem: The original graph-product hypotheses include trace-zero unitaries in vertex algebras. Such unitaries may be usable, via graph-product normal forms, to build expectation-vanishing sequences that rule out relative amenability or intertwining into certain proper parabolic subalgebras.

other_useful_info: The paper explicitly notes several reusable hypotheses: subgroup inclusions LΛ⊂LΓ always have bounded Pimsner-Popa bases from coset representatives; almost malnormality of Λ<Γ implies the inclusion LΛ⊂LΓ is mixing; and free-product factors M_i⊂M_1∗M_2 are mixing when the factors are diffuse. The free product applications rely on the known fact that Γ_1∗Γ_2 is bi-exact relative to {Γ_1,Γ_2} when Γ_i are exact. The authors state that for general tracial free products M_1∗M_2 it should be true that subalgebras without amenable direct summands are properly proximal relative to the boundary piece generated by M_1 and M_2, but in this paper they only obtain the needed result under the extra assumption M_i=LΓ_i with Γ_i exact. They also mention the folklore conjecture, stated by Oyakawa, that exact relatively hyperbolic groups are bi-exact relative to their peripheral subgroups; peripheral subgroups are almost malnormal, so Theorem 1.2 would apply if that conjecture holds.
why_relevant: Develops von Neumann algebra-level techniques for upgrading relative proper proximality to full proper proximality using bidual computations. This is directly relevant if the graph-product proof first establishes proper proximality relative to link/star subalgebras and then uses irreducibility to upgrade.
